english_name: The First Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Corinthians

verses.latin_vulgate: De collectis autem, quæ fiunt in sanctos, sicut ordinavi ecclesiis Galatiæ, ita et vos facite.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: περὶ δὲ τῆς λογίας τῆς εἰς τοὺς ἁγίους ὥσπερ διέταξα ταῖς ἐκκλησίαις τῆς γαλατίας οὕτως καὶ ὑμεῖς ποιήσατε
verses.king_james: Now concerning the collection for the saints, as I have given order to the churches of Galatia, even so do ye.

verses.latin_vulgate: Per unam sabbati unusquisque vestrum apud se seponat, recondens quod ei bene placuerit: ut non, cum venero, tunc collectæ fiant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κατὰ μίαν σαββάτων ἕκαστος ὑμῶν παρ᾽ ἑαυτῷ τιθέτω θησαυρίζων ὅ τι ἂν εὐοδῶται ἵνα μὴ ὅταν ἔλθω τότε λογίαι γίνωνται
verses.king_james: Upon the first *day* of the week let every one of you lay by him in store, as *God* hath prospered him, that there be no gatherings when I come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem præsens fuero, quos probaveritis per epistolas, hos mittam perferre gratiam vestram in Jerusalem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν δὲ παραγένωμαι οὓς ἐὰν δοκιμάσητε δι᾽ ἐπιστολῶν τούτους πέμψω ἀπενεγκεῖν τὴν χάριν ὑμῶν εἰς ἰερουσαλήμ
verses.king_james: And when I come, whomsoever ye shall approve by *your* letters, them will I send to bring your liberality unto Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod si dignum fuerit ut et ego eam, mecum ibunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν δὲ ᾖ ἄξιον τοῦ κἀμὲ πορεύεσθαι σὺν ἐμοὶ πορεύσονται
verses.king_james: And if it be meet that I go also, they shall go with me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Veniam autem ad vos, cum Macedoniam pertransiero: nam Macedoniam pertransibo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐλεύσομαι δὲ πρὸς ὑμᾶς ὅταν Μακεδονίαν διέλθω Μακεδονίαν γὰρ διέρχομαι
verses.king_james: Now I will come unto you, when I shall pass through Macedonia: for I do pass through Macedonia.

verses.latin_vulgate: Apud vos autem forsitan manebo, vel etiam hiemabo: ut vos me deducatis quocumque iero.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πρὸς ὑμᾶς δὲ τυχὸν παραμενῶ ἢ καὶ παραχειμάσω ἵνα ὑμεῖς με προπέμψητε οὗ ἐὰν πορεύωμαι
verses.king_james: And it may be that I will abide, yea, and winter with you, that ye may bring me on my journey whithersoever I go.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nolo enim vos modo in transitu videre, spero enim me aliquantulum temporis manere apud vos, si Dominus permiserit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ θέλω γὰρ ὑμᾶς ἄρτι ἐν παρόδῳ ἰδεῖν ἐλπίζω δὲ χρόνον τινὰ ἐπιμεῖναι πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἐὰν ὁ κύριος ἐπιτρέπῃ
verses.king_james: For I will not see you now by the way; but I trust to tarry a while with you, if the Lord permit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Permanebo autem Ephesi usque ad Pentecosten.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπιμενῶ δὲ ἐν ἐφέσῳ ἕως τῆς πεντηκοστῆς
verses.king_james: But I will tarry at Ephesus until Pentecost.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ostium enim mihi apertum est magnum, et evidens: et adversarii multi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θύρα γάρ μοι ἀνέῳγεν μεγάλη καὶ ἐνεργής καὶ ἀντικείμενοι πολλοί
verses.king_james: For a great door and effectual is opened unto me, and *there are* many adversaries.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem venerit Timotheus, videte ut sine timore sit apud vos: opus enim Domini operatur, sicut et ego.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν δὲ ἔλθῃ τιμόθεος βλέπετε ἵνα ἀφόβως γένηται πρὸς ὑμᾶς τὸ γὰρ ἔργον κυρίου ἐργάζεται ὡς καὶ ἐγώ
verses.king_james: Now if Timotheus come, see that he may be with you without fear: for he worketh the work of the Lord, as I also *do*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne quis ergo illum spernat: deducite autem illum in pace, ut veniat ad me: exspecto enim illum cum fratribus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μή τις οὖν αὐτὸν ἐξουθενήσῃ προπέμψατε δὲ αὐτὸν ἐν εἰρήνῃ ἵνα ἔλθῃ πρός με ἐκδέχομαι γὰρ αὐτὸν μετὰ τῶν ἀδελφῶν
verses.king_james: Let no man therefore despise him: but conduct him forth in peace, that he may come unto me: for I look for him with the brethren.

verses.latin_vulgate: De Apollo autem fratre vobis notum facio, quoniam multum rogavi eum ut veniret ad vos cum fratribus: et utique non fuit voluntas ut nunc veniret: veniet autem, cum ei vacuum fuerit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: περὶ δὲ ἀπολλῶ τοῦ ἀδελφοῦ πολλὰ παρεκάλεσα αὐτὸν ἵνα ἔλθῃ πρὸς ὑμᾶς μετὰ τῶν ἀδελφῶν καὶ πάντως οὐκ ἦν θέλημα ἵνα νῦν ἔλθῃ ἐλεύσεται δὲ ὅταν εὐκαιρήσῃ
verses.king_james: As touching *our* brother Apollos, I greatly desired him to come unto you with the brethren: but his will was not at all to come at this time; but he will come when he shall have convenient time.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vigilate, state in fide, viriliter agite, et confortamini.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γρηγορεῖτε στήκετε ἐν τῇ πίστει ἀνδρίζεσθε κραταιοῦσθε
verses.king_james: Watch ye, stand fast in the faith, quit you like men, be strong.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnia vestra in caritate fiant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντα ὑμῶν ἐν ἀγάπῃ γινέσθω
verses.king_james: Let all your things be done with charity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Obsecro autem vos fratres, nostis domum Stephanæ, et Fortunati, et Achaici: quoniam sunt primitiæ Achaiæ, et in ministerium sanctorum ordinaverunt seipsos:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παρακαλῶ δὲ ὑμᾶς ἀδελφοί οἴδατε τὴν οἰκίαν στεφανᾶ ὅτι ἐστὶν ἀπαρχὴ τῆς ἀχαΐας καὶ εἰς διακονίαν τοῖς ἁγίοις ἔταξαν ἑαυτούς
verses.king_james: I beseech you, brethren, (ye know the house of Stephanas, that it is the firstfruits of Achaia, and *that* they have addicted themselves to the ministry of the saints,)

verses.latin_vulgate: ut et vos subditi sitis ejusmodi, et omni cooperanti, et laboranti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα καὶ ὑμεῖς ὑποτάσσησθε τοῖς τοιούτοις καὶ παντὶ τῷ συνεργοῦντι καὶ κοπιῶντι
verses.king_james: That ye submit yourselves unto such, and to every one that helpeth with *us*, and laboureth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gaudeo autem in præsentia Stephanæ, et Fortunati, et Achaici: quoniam id, quod vobis deerat, ipsi suppleverunt:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: χαίρω δὲ ἐπὶ τῇ παρουσίᾳ στεφανᾶ καὶ φουρτουνάτου καὶ ἀχαϊκοῦ ὅτι τὸ ὑμῶν ὑστέρημα οὗτοι ἀνεπλήρωσαν
verses.king_james: I am glad of the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus: for that which was lacking on your part they have supplied.

verses.latin_vulgate: refecerunt enim et meum spiritum, et vestrum. Cognoscite ergo qui hujusmodi sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀνέπαυσαν γὰρ τὸ ἐμὸν πνεῦμα καὶ τὸ ὑμῶν ἐπιγινώσκετε οὖν τοὺς τοιούτους
verses.king_james: For they have refreshed my spirit and yours: therefore acknowledge ye them that are such.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutant vos ecclesiæ Asiæ. Salutant vos in Domino multum, Aquila et Priscilla cum domestica sua ecclesia: apud quos et hospitor.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάζονται ὑμᾶς αἱ ἐκκλησίαι τῆς ἀσίας ἀσπάζονται ὑμᾶς ἐν κυρίῳ πολλὰ ἀκύλας καὶ πρίσκιλλα, σὺν τῇ κατ᾽ οἶκον αὐτῶν ἐκκλησίᾳ
verses.king_james: The churches of Asia salute you. Aquila and Priscilla salute you much in the Lord, with the church that is in their house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutant vos omnes fratres. Salutate invicem in osculo sancto.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάζονται ὑμᾶς οἱ ἀδελφοὶ πάντες ἀσπάσασθε ἀλλήλους ἐν φιλήματι ἁγίῳ
verses.king_james: All the brethren greet you. Greet ye one another with an holy kiss.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutatio, mea manu Pauli.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ ἀσπασμὸς τῇ ἐμῇ χειρὶ παύλου
verses.king_james: The salutation of *me* Paul with mine own hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quis non amat Dominum nostrum Jesum Christum, sit anathema, Maran Atha.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴ τις οὐ φιλεῖ τὸν κύριον ἰησοῦν χριστόν, ἤτω ἀνάθεμα μαρὰν ἀθά
verses.king_james: If any man love not the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be Anathema Maranatha.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gratia Domini nostri Jesu Christi vobiscum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ χάρις τοῦ κυρίου ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ μεθ᾽ ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ *be* with you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Caritas mea cum omnibus vobis in Christo Jesu. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ ἀγάπη μου μετὰ πάντων ὑμῶν ἐν χριστῷ ἰησοῦ ἀμήν πρὸς κορινθίους πρώτη ἐγράφη ἀπὸ φιλίππων διὰ στεφανᾶ καὶ φουρτουνάτου καὶ ἀχαϊκοῦ καὶ τιμοθέου
verses.king_james: My love *be* with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen.
